params.name: Torricelli's Barometer
params.description:
On May 8, 1643 AD, Torricelli collaborated with Viviani to create the world's first mercury barometer.
The mercury barometer is Torricelli's most important invention. As a student and assistant of Galileo, he strongly supported Galileo's theories and experiments. In an experiment to test the existence of a vacuum, he noticed that, after filling a glass tube with mercury, the mercury would fall to the same level, regardless of the tube's length or angle. This discovery led to the method of mea …
